name: Noah Kahan
why: The Great Divide debuted at No. 1 on the Billboard 200 in May 2026 and spent three straight weeks at the top, posting the biggest streaming week of any rock album in the modern chart era.

name: Morgan Wallen
why: I'm the Problem holds at No. 3 on the Billboard 200 the week of June 13, 2026, sustaining a months-long run as the dominant country act across all-genre charts.

name: Kendrick Lamar
why: Won five Grammys at the 2026 ceremony, including Best Rap Album for GNX, breaking Jay-Z's record for most Grammy wins by a rapper with 27 total trophies.

name: Paul McCartney
why: The Boys of Dungeon Lane, his first solo studio album in over five years, debuted at No. 1 on Top Album Sales and No. 5 on the Billboard 200 the week of June 13, 2026.

name: Shakira
why: Co-performed the official 2026 FIFA World Cup anthem Dai Dai with Burna Boy at the Estadio Azteca opening ceremony on June 11 before 80,000 fans, and will headline the first-ever World Cup final halftime show at MetLife Stadium on July 19.

name: Burna Boy
why: Co-wrote and performed Dai Dai, the official 2026 FIFA World Cup anthem, with Shakira. The track debuted live at the Estadio Azteca opening ceremony on June 11, 2026, broadcast globally.

name: Jennie
why: Dracula, her Tame Impala collaboration, climbed to No. 10 on the Billboard Hot 100 the week of June 13, 2026, her first solo top 10 entry and making BLACKPINK the first K-pop group with two members holding solo top-10 singles.

name: Elyanna
why: The singer of Palestinian and Chilean heritage headlined the 2026 FIFA World Cup Canada opening ceremony in Toronto on June 12, 2026, becoming the first Arabic-language pop act to perform at a FIFA tournament opening.
signal: FIFA World Cup 2026 Canada opening ceremony

name: LISA
why: Performed at the 2026 FIFA World Cup USA opening ceremony at Los Angeles Stadium on June 12, 2026, alongside Katy Perry and Anitta, consolidating her solo global reach after the crossover success of Rockstar.
signal: FIFA World Cup 2026 USA opening ceremony

name: Avery Singer
why: Her show War_overlays opened at Zurich Gallery Weekend in June 2026, using AI to study how mass media shapes consciousness around violence. Her auction market is surging in 2026, with collectors and institutions driving significant new demand for her work.

name: Shuang Li
why: Opened a solo exhibition at Kunsthalle Basel in June 2026, part of a rapidly growing international museum profile for the Chinese artist whose practice spans conceptual performance, text, and institutional critique.

name: Yelena Popova
why: Her solo show at Osnova Gallery in Valencia runs through July 16, 2026, presenting new works that fuse scientific research, historical inquiry, and poetic imagination to address climate change and ecological fragility.

name: Zendaya
why: Four major studio films are scheduled for 2026, including Christopher Nolan's The Odyssey, Dune 3, Spider-Man: Brand New Day, and Shrek 5, making her the most in-demand film actress in the world this year.

name: Clint419
why: Founder of Corteiz, the most disruptive UK streetwear brand of 2026. The label's collaboration with the Virgil Abloh Archives was the year's most talked-about fashion drop, with a Nike Shox R419 collab expected later in 2026.

name: Nora Fatehi
why: Dancer and entertainer Nora Fatehi co-headlined the 2026 FIFA World Cup Canada opening ceremony in Toronto on June 12, 2026, reaching a prime-time global broadcast audience and extending her presence from Bollywood to international stages.
